summary: All 7 spec ACs (R1a, R1b, R2a, R2b, R3, R4, build/test) map 1:1 to exactly one bead with a named RED test in the Provenance table; no double-mapping, no orphan bead work. Seams verified against source: gitutil execCommand (gitops.go:243/277/298 are the 3 named network ops; env unset today = RED), doctor bdSchemaDriftRE (beads.go:326 'could not be found'-only => new phrasings RED) + checkMultipleBdOnPath symlink-dedup test is genuinely NEW (existing _SingleBinary uses one dir, never exercises EvalSymlinks/seenResolved), next.SelectWorkByName (select.go:38 exact == => short-form RED), phase.fetchChildren (cache.go:195 hardcoded open,in_progress,closed => blocked omitted; the captured --status argv assertion is the real RED, child-presence alone is a weak proxy and the plan correctly flags this). bead.AllStatuses(root) BuiltinStatuses() always contains 'blocked' so R4 test is robust under stubbed listJSONFn regardless of root resolution. Minor non-blocking notes (not CHANGES): (1) R3 AC bundles both short+full forms but only short-form is RED today — full form already passes via item.ID==name; acceptable since the test pins both. (2) R4 plan itself notes child-presence is a weak proxy and pins the --status breadth argv as the load-bearing assertion — correctly handled. Every bead does only work an AC asked for.

ac_map.ac: R1: FetchRemote/PushBranch/DetectDefaultBranch remote-show run with GIT_TERMINAL_PROMPT=0 in cmd.Env; gitutil unit test via execCommand seam asserts it
ac_map.red_test: NEW env-capture test (execCommand seam extended to retain .Env)
ac_map.verdict: exercises AC: asserts env entry on the 3 named ops; RED (no op sets env today)

ac_map.ac: R1: non-prompting success path unchanged (fallback only on non-zero exit)
ac_map.red_test: existing TestFetchRemote / TestDefaultBranch_* stay green (regression guard)
ac_map.verdict: GREEN-guard, correct shape for a no-regression AC

ac_map.ac: R2: bdSchemaDriftRE true for no such column / unknown column / Error 1054, false for unrelated transient
ac_map.verdict: exercises AC directly; RED (current regex is could-not-be-found-only); conservative-false case pinned

ac_map.ac: R2: single bd via two PATH entries (real dir + symlink/dup) => OK, no Warn
ac_map.red_test: NEW symlink-dedup test (fakeBdDir + os.Symlink + t.Setenv PATH)
ac_map.verdict: genuinely NEW — existing _SingleBinary uses one dir and never hits EvalSymlinks/seenResolved; this directly exercises the dedup guards

ac_map.ac: R3: SelectWorkByName(items,xxxx) and (items,mindspec-xxxx) both resolve to mindspec-xxxx; neither-form => not-in-ready-set error, no fall-through to items[0]
ac_map.red_test: NEW both-forms test + not-found test extending TestSelectWorkByName_*
ac_map.verdict: exercises AC; short-form is the RED case (full form already passes today); no-fall-through pinned

ac_map.ac: R4: cache test stubs listJSONFn so a blocked child is included; query covers bead.AllStatuses breadth
ac_map.red_test: NEW blocked-child test + --status argv breadth assertion; unchanged _MemoizesPerEpic
ac_map.verdict: child-presence alone is a weak proxy (stub returns it regardless) — plan correctly identifies the captured --status argv (contains blocked) as the load-bearing RED assertion; single-call constraint guarded by _MemoizesPerEpic

ac_map.ac: go build ./... passes; the four added/changed tests pass under filtered go test -run <Name>
ac_map.verdict: covered by each bead's Verification section
